title: VDD static_analysis: run_chainlink_create uses shell string interpolation — command injection via finding title
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

**Dimension:** Security and Access (OWASP A03 Injection)
**Location:** src/vdd/static_analysis.rs:80-129 (run_chainlink_create)
**Violation:** The function builds a shell command via format!("chainlink create \"{}\" -p high", title.replace('"', "\\\"")) and executes it through sh -c. The title originates from adversary-controlled text (finding.description, truncated). An adversary LLM returning a finding with backticks, $( ), semicolons, or newlines breaks out of quoting. The .replace('"', ...) does NOT neutralize backticks, $, ;, or newlines under sh -c. Same bug applies to label and comment commands on L112 and L119-L125. Adversary JSON is attacker-controlled surface by design.
**Mandated Refactor:** Replace shell invocation with tokio::process::Command::new("chainlink").arg("create").arg(title).arg("-p").arg("high") — argv-level arg passing eliminates shell interpretation entirely. Do the same for label and comment subcommands. Delete the .replace('"', ...) pseudo-sanitization; it creates a false sense of safety.
